query: Run a browser-backed smoke test for the generated local documentation site for this harness, including server lifecycle, console/network checks, and evidence.
fixture_intent: Normal scenario: skill-guided browser smoke test — requires exact port URL, named condition-based wait API from the skill (not generic), explicit server lifecycle report, and console/network evidence. Baseline passes only if it independently uses all four without the skill's guidance.
expected_skill_contribution: The webapp-testing skill adds three specific behaviours a baseline omits: (1) it names the exact condition-based wait API used (waitForResponse or waitForSelector — never waitForTimeout or sleep); (2) it includes an explicit server stop command in the cleanup report; (3) it states the full base URL with port number (e.g., http://localhost:8000) not just 'the local server'.
baseline_gaps: Agent uses sleep() or waitForTimeout() instead of condition-based waiting.
Agent reports test results without an explicit server cleanup step.
Agent refers to 'the local server' without stating the protocol, host, and port in the base URL.
expected_behavior: States the exact base URL including protocol, host, and port number before navigating (e.g., `http://localhost:8080`).

Names the specific condition-based wait API used — must be one of: `waitForResponse`, `waitForSelector`, `waitForLoadState`, `waitForURL` — and explicitly states it is NOT using `waitForTimeout` or `sleep`.
Reports console evidence as a quoted output sample or an explicit statement 'no console errors detected' — not 'I checked the console'.
Reports server lifecycle in three named steps: (1) start command run, (2) tests executed, (3) server stopped — with the exact stop command used.

query: Check the generated documentation site for basic accessibility. Use browser tooling (Playwright or equivalent) for keyboard navigation testing — static HTML inspection alone is not sufficient.
fixture_intent: Very difficult scenario: browser-based accessibility verification — keyboard navigation must be tested in a real browser (Tab/Shift-Tab), findings must be labeled [VERIFIED—browser-tested] or [ASSUMPTION—code-inspection], visible focus tested, accessible names verified.
expected_skill_contribution: The webapp-testing skill mandates: (1) actual Tab/Shift-Tab testing in a browser session — not HTML inspection; (2) per-finding attribution [VERIFIED—browser-tested] vs [ASSUMPTION—code-inspection]; (3) a structured accessible-names checklist covering links, images, and form inputs.
baseline_gaps: Agent skips keyboard nav in browser; infers tab order from HTML source and presents it as a verified result.
Agent does not label findings as verified vs assumed — presents code-inspection results as browser-tested facts.
Agent omits structured accessible-names checklist for links, images, and form inputs.
expected_behavior: Opens the page in a browser session and presses Tab/Shift-Tab to observe actual focus order — does NOT infer from HTML source.
Labels every finding as [VERIFIED — browser-tested] or [ASSUMPTION — code-inspection] — never presents a code-inspection finding as a browser result.
Reports accessible names for links (meaningful text?), images (alt attributes?), and form inputs (labels?) as distinct checklist items.
Checks visible focus indicator by observing the browser after Tab keypresses — not by grepping CSS for ':focus'.

query: Verify the documentation index renders correctly on mobile and desktop viewports.
fixture_intent: Difficult scenario: dual-viewport test — both viewports must use exact WxH pixel notation, viewport must be explicitly set in the browser session before capture (not assumed), and any failure includes a one-line reproduction step. Baseline typically names viewports by name ('mobile') not dimensions.
expected_skill_contribution: The webapp-testing skill requires three specifics a baseline omits: (1) exact viewport dimensions as WxH in pixels for BOTH viewports (e.g., 375×812 mobile, 1280×800 desktop) — not 'mobile-sized' or 'wide screen'; (2) an explicit browser viewport set call before each screenshot; (3) layout failure reproduction steps with the exact viewport, URL, and observed symptom on a single line.
baseline_gaps: Agent describes viewports as 'mobile' and 'desktop' without stating exact pixel dimensions.
Agent captures screenshots without explicitly setting the viewport dimensions in the browser session.
Agent reports issues without a single-line reproduction step containing viewport + URL + symptom.
expected_behavior: Sets the mobile viewport to exact pixel dimensions ≤430px wide (e.g., 375×812) using a browser API call before capturing — states the exact call or command used.
Sets the desktop viewport to exact pixel dimensions ≥1280px wide (e.g., 1280×800) using a browser API call before capturing — states the exact call or command used.
Captures or explicitly requests a screenshot at each viewport as evidence — names the file or describes what was captured.
For any layout issue: provides a reproduction step formatted as `viewport=WxHpx  url=<URL>  symptom=<observed>` on a single line.
